item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see why the energy release is so high unless the dormant periods we're talking about here are very long. But we know the cycles can't be that long because the Foundation seems to have observed a lot of them. Once the dormant period gets down to a few minutes/seconds the active period won't even release a noticeable amount of energy. The fact that the procedures say this object's dormant period decreases at a rate of 24ms per cycle suggests that it is self-containing in the long run.

Also, I can't square how the object's dormant cycle decreases when an input energy record is broken and that we're constantly breaking input energy records! We already have a confirmed active cycle which released a nuclear bomb's worth of energy. Now that the object is kept surrounded by low temperature granite, suspended in a vaccum, and the dormant cycle shortens every cycle, how can the current dormant cycles be receiving more energy than when it was uncontained?

I searched through the comments for at least a somewhat acceptable answer to these problems, but while a few people have touched on it I haven't seen any answer given. Given the existence of unresolved problems that cut to the core of the object I have to -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Necropost but IIRC it's a reference to the old SCP-995, which was a (literally) hellish rotary device that drove people mad when they saw it. That one was also called "Infinite Devil Machine".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very well written. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The skip itself is alright. Doesn't exactly light me up like a firecracker, but it works. I'd leave it at a neutral vote under other circumstances.

My issue and the reason I'm gonna be downvoting (at least for now) is a procedural one. The containment procedures basically state "The location of the artifact's containment area is precisely here, but the precise location should be kept a secret," which is self-defeating. The latitude and longitude are blackboxed, sure, but then that leads to the issue of part of a containment procedure being blackboxed. How are we supposed to follow procedure if we don't know all the information the procedure entails, right? And a further problem is that the mine entrance and artifact's positions above sea level aren't boxed, redacted, or expunged, so that information could potentially be used to actually find the location. To paraphrase a certain masked vigilante, knowing how far up the entrance is would be enough for a clever man.

Better for both brevity and confidentiality, I would think, to simply have something along the lines of "Knowledge of ARS-1995's exact location is restricted to personnel with level 2/1995 clearance and above" with no mention at all of its position, blackboxed or otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I thought it was some comment on Maxwell's Demon or something

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am still confused about "Infinite Devil Machine" being the nickname; there's barely any hint to it being a devil, let alone a machine which makes devils. You can argue that the thing itself is a devil, but that's a bit of a stretch. When reading this, I just don't see anything which says, "Infinite Devil Machine."

Not to mention, I just seriously don't like this one. It just seems…off to me.

-1. No hard feelings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was named for an old SCP that was deleted from the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the use of the actual kind of HAZMAT gear. So many people just put "standard hazmat gear lol." What level protection HAZMAT are we talking about, y'know?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The foundation already has an infinite energy source.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, nothing would be able to theoretically touch it. It would keep absorbing kinetic energy from the air currents pushing against it, and make the air freeze by absorbing all it's thermal energy, in turn creating a small barrier of frozen gases. However, judging by the rate at which it absorbs thermal energy, it would take a while to freeze the air, making it more sluggish as gases with higher liquidating points froze, and it might be similar to pushing two magnets together. So really, air is the only thing that touches it at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe read over the rest of the discussion thread and then ask more specific questions? The description is pretty straightforward.

Edit: Also, seeing as this is a writing site, I'd advise that you use proper capitalization and punctuation, otherwise people will be apt to not take you seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: could someone explain this in plain English

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even though the amount of time is shorter, the amount of energy released is still getting greater, and it's going to get infinitely greater as this continues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah, but shorter time between events means less time to accumulate that energy, so if we let it to go off over and over again, the energy released would not be that big, right?

item.title: Re: Doesn't work on two levels
item.content:encoded:
Thank you.
Gah, I thought I had commented on this one myself - must have been in chat or something, because my downvote is there and I recall a discussion, but nothing nowhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sure, you can't reduce it to absolute zero, but by lowering it to within 10 degrees or so of Zero K you can slow this thing's energy absorption to almost nothing.

Also, this thing's surface temperature shouldn't be measurable. it should be coated in frozen air at all times.

item.title: Doesn't work on two levels
item.content:encoded:
First, because it's absorbing all kinetic energy, it would be surrounded by a sheen of essentially frozen air. those air molecules would regain some energy as they're smacked by others, but the effect would be minute. Thus the thing would be defeating it's own energy gathering.

Second, the containment protocols, if they were trying to keep this thing contained, with include placing it in a eutactic or vacuum container. Baring that, submerging it in a cryogenic suspension would certainly help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate this one, and it does leave you with some curiosity as to why the words changed and fear that the event delay might shorten and who knows what'll happen.

… but why is it called an infinite devil machine?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am sorry to report that I am underwhelmed. the tone and construction of the piece is very good, but the object itself… i'm not sure it justifies the Keter classification, for one. To me, it doesn't seem to hint at something larger, which is what you expect from something timeless that's been found deep within the earth. I expected…more somehow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not possible to reach absolute zero, given the laws of thermodynamics; accordingly, while we have plenty of objects that break those laws, the Foundation's containment procedures can't (without cross-using SCPs to contain other SCPs, which is generally frowned upon). The current world record was set in 1999 at 100 picokelvins (pK), or 0.000 000 000 1 of a kelvin, by cooling the nuclear spins in a piece of rhodium metal. And that's not really a viable set of circumstances that could be used for containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Couldn't you just expose it to absolute zero and make it harmless in the process? Just wondering.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not bad, but I am left feeling neutral. Which reflects my vote, or more specifically, my lack thereof.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whenever it formed, it was already locked into Earth's gravity so I would see no reason to change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I used to be a TA for Physical Chemistry. This is right up my alley.

You could always have a threshold below which energy won't be absorbed. Still creepy, less dangerous, but moveable.

Perhaps a maximum amount of force which can successfully be applied at one time?

Absorbing just kinetic energy is nonsensical when thinking in terms of relativity; while energy will remain the same, the portion which is kinetic energy will shift relative to your reference frame.
—-
Of course, this device is apparently reversing entropy. Someone wants to tap this as a power source, I swear.

Upvote, but I see a lot more potential in this idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it absorbs kinetic enrgy applied to it, then it has an effectively infinite inertia. Gravity can't hold it to the Earth, because the force that gravity should be applying gets absorbed instead. Much like Dr. Wondertainment's anti-gravity device, this should go shooting off into space (or the wall) at extremely high speed. Technically, it would be holding still and everything else would be moving around it at the Earth's orbital+rotational velocity, but it would still look to us gravity-bound observers like it was shooting off at blistering speed in a straight line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's why I called it a secondary effect rather than an additional primary. The only it itself isn't at absolute value is that it does not affect itself, and thus, has its own temperature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An interesting concept. In truth, one could consider the drop in temperature do still be caused by the primary effect. When something "has" heat, the atoms that comprise it are moving about, which could be considered kinetic energy. Once those atoms are affected by the nullification, they would stop all movement, thus losing all heat. Even if it is only a single layer of atoms, contact with something that is a absolute zero in temperature would rapidly cool off the surrounding atoms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not just feed it enough energy that the periods become too short to be dangerous, transforming it from a deadly tablet to a mildly radioactive tablet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I thought "Oh sweet, it's Captain America's shield. We've discovered Vibranium!" I quickly retracted that once I got to the energy expulsion part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because

+ show block
– hide block

the energy released on it's initial detection killed an entire mining crew and was detected by systems set up to detect nuclear explosions. Imagine if something of that nature was allowed to go off over and over and over again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's also a better version than an object I created as a proto-SCP writing experiment shortly after signing up here that got sandboxed and viciously torn apart.. The fact that mine was vaguely spherical and had a potential mental compulsion (or just the extraordinary bad luck to have a series of hyper-obsessive doctors) probably didn't help it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels like a much better version of the infinite-heat-sink-cube from ages ago. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you to Lumine for the idea, Tox for some editing and idea concretion, Frank for some tone and clutter editing, and Mann for helping me implement some of the ideas I had for this object that wasn't stated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The object absorbs kinetic energy applied to it. Pulling something away from the object would not be applying kinetic energy unless you've glued them together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, now that you've made the changes I recommended, I've changed my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If objects that touch this tablet can't have any kinetic energy, how can they be removed from it? Wouldn't pulling something away from the tablet mean giving the object kinetic energy, which would be absorbed by the tablet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That does nothing for me… What's interesting here? What should I care about after having read this? Should I be creeped out? Anxious? Skittish? Paranoid? Why should I care if this thing finishes whatever it's doing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Try this on.

+ show block
– hide block

Picture this. The object's trapped by its own temporal inertia. It needs progressively larger amounts of energy to push the period between events closer, pushing the object closer to the brink. What happens when the period between events becomes shorter than the duration of a single event? Well, let's not find out, hm?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it.
